clean_body: | Hi Peter, Adelita Grijalva. I want to thank Jonathan Nez for giving me the chance to reach out to you directly. Last month, voters overwhelmingly rejected the MAGA agenda and chose me to represent them in Congress in Arizona's 7th District special election. With so much at stake, I came to Washington ready to get to work. But instead of swearing me in, Speaker Mike Johnson cancelled session and sent members home -- not only delaying my ability to take office, but also allowing the government to shut down without even letting the House work toward a solution. That's how far Republicans are willing to go to block me from serving. Republicans know that once I'm sworn in, it won't just chip away at their razor-thin majority -- I will become the decisive 218th signature to force a vote on releasing the Epstein files. This is nothing more than a desperate power play to protect Trump while denying the people of my district a voice in Congress when it's needed the most. As Speaker Johnson drags this out, we're going to prove that our grassroots power can't be silenced. Each day he continues to delay, we've set a goal of raising $10,000 to keep our momentum going as we work to take back the House in 2026. ~~ | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your split contribution will process now. | ~ | ~ | ~ | ~ | ~ | ~ Juntos, Adelita S. Steve Bannon, one of Trump's staunchest allies and strategists, told a national newspaper that Trump is "a vehicle of divine providence" and "there is a plan" for a Trump third term in 2028.
The plan Bannon is referring to likely hinges on MAGA seizing control over Pennsylvania's Supreme Court on November 4th.
If MAGA succeeds in toppling the 3 Democrats on the court, Trump will control the court that decides how elections are run in the most important swing state in America, allowing him rig the upcoming '28 election in his favor--banning mail-in voting, invalidating Democratic ballots, and extreme suppression laws.
This would all but guarantee the White House for Trump when he runs for a third term in 2028.
---

name: Nico, United We Dream
subject: They’re betting on us to stay scared and divided
body:
You can help immigrant youth keep their status by donating to the DACA Renewal Fund today. | | | Peter, DACA recipients — and all immigrants — should be able to live with dignity and safety. But right now, DACA recipients and immigrant youth are being cruelly targeted, abducted, and disappeared. During these uncertain times, DACA recipients must navigate a complex immigration system to renew their DACA status. [**The cost of renewing DACA has increased to $555 online, and even more for paper applications. clean_body: You can help immigrant youth keep their status by donating to the DACA Renewal Fund today. | | | Peter, DACA recipients — and all immigrants — should be able to live with dignity and safety. But right now, DACA recipients and immigrant youth are being cruelly targeted, abducted, and disappeared. During these uncertain times, DACA recipients must navigate a complex immigration system to renew their DACA status. | | Food assistance for low-income families is being ripped away, and people across the country are set to lose access to affordable health care. Meanwhile, taxpayer dollars are being used to terrorize our communities. They’re betting on us to stay scared and divided so their crusade against the people who power this country can continue. It’s up to us to stand with and for each other. Our DACA renewal fund continues to support immigrant youth, but resources are limited and demand is growing as more DACA recipients turn to us for help renewing their DACA status. Your donation, of any amount, will help us send a message to immigrant youth: Your home is here. name: Eric Swalwell
subject: Republicans are blocking justice and ignoring voters
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Swalwell For Congress
body:
Democrat Eric Swalwell represents California's 14th Congressional District.
https://ericswalwell.com [https://ericswalwell.com]
Friend -

Adelita Grijalva won a clear, decisive victory in Arizona’s special election, but instead of honoring the will of Arizona voters, Mike Johnson and Republicans are doing everything possible to keep her from being sworn in.

body:
Your trust in me means the world.

| **It’s pretty clear that Donald Trump is afraid of me.**

Trump has publicly endorsed my opponent (after Jack shamelessly begged for his support). He’s attacked me by name in multiple unhinged rants on social media. His administration _illegally leaked my private records to Jack’s campaign ally._

But Peter, Trump is even more afraid of the movement we’ve built together here in New Jersey — because if we win, we will send a powerful message to the nation that the people do not support his destructive and incompetent presidency.

**That’s why he’s doing everything he can to stop us from winning this upcoming election, and is rallying the support of his deep-pocketed allies to flood this race with spending against us.**

[_**Donald Trump knows that as governor, I’m going to do everything in my power to fight back against his disastrous agenda. But polling shows a neck-and-neck race, Peter, which means we absolutely can’t let Trump’s attacks go unanswered. name: Mark Warner
subject: a quick note less than 2 weeks out from Election Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Mark Warner
body:
|
---

| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117986678/578279558/1668162259?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_OneWeekRsnd_251025_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NjUyOTU5MC1iYWIxLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jNr86rp4QkCR4ZrRPfiT5Vbqb4MSADrf1WcwHmC2MGA=) _We understand that many_ _Americans_ _are directly impacted by the government shutdown. If you would like to pause emails from Team Warner until the federal government reopens,[_**please let us know by completing this form**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117986679/578279559/222818858?refcode=MW_ATA_EM_FR_OneWeekRsnd_251025_T1_1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI3NjUyOTU5MC1iYWIxLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=jNr86rp4QkCR4ZrRPfiT5Vbqb4MSADrf1WcwHmC2MGA=&emci=503062b3-19b1-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=76529590-bab1-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=4568237)._ Election Day in Virginia is less than two weeks away, Derek.

Voters will decide whether to expand our razor-thin Democratic majority in the legislature and flip the Governorship from Republican to Democratic.

I could give you a list of Democratic candidates I believe deserve your support – frankly, there’s a lot of them, Derek.

Instead, I simply want to say that **these results will have far reaching consequences for the Commonwealth.**

Think about it: Trump has driven up unemployment, fired thousands of federal workers, and he continues to put Virginians' livelihoods at risk, all while cutting taxes for the wealthiest Americans.

While I lead the fight in Washington to hold him accountable for his chaos and corruption, I know I can count on my fellow Democrats in Richmond to do the same. But that fight would change dramatically if Republicans gain more control of the Commonwealth or defeat me next year.

**The very last thing we need is for Trump to buy his allies even more power in Virginia and Washington.**

[_**So I’m asking: will you pitch in to my reelection campaign today to keep Virginia blue and protect this Senate seat so we can keep up the fight to hold the Trump administration clean_body: | | | = We understand that many Americans are directly impacted by the government shutdown. If you would like to pause emails from Team Warner until the federal government reopens,. Election Day in Virginia is less than two weeks away, Derek. Voters will decide whether to expand our razor-thin Democratic majority in the legislature and flip the Governorship from Republican to Democratic. I could give you a list of Democratic candidates I believe deserve your support – frankly, there’s a lot of them, Derek. Instead, I simply want to say that these results will have far reaching consequences for the Commonwealth. Think about it: Trump has driven up unemployment, fired thousands of federal workers, and he continues to put Virginians' livelihoods at risk, all while cutting taxes for the wealthiest Americans. While I lead the fight in Washington to hold him accountable for his chaos and corruption, I know I can count on my fellow Democrats in Richmond to do the same. But that fight would change dramatically if Republicans gain more control of the Commonwealth or defeat me next year. The very last thing we need is for Trump to buy his allies even more power in Virginia and Washington. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately: = = = = = = The fight continues, Mark Warner Before you go: Mark Warner is leading the fight to hold the Trump administration accountable – but that could make his race a top Republican target next year. United for Democracy
* * *
Peter,
Since Congress shut down on September 30, federal workers have either been off the job or working without pay. Today, they just missed their first paycheck.
Instead of negotiating an end to the shutdown, Donald Trump and his administration have threatened mass firings, even pledging to renege on a law _he signed_ to guarantee backpay to workers after a shutdown. **Trump sees a path to political victory through punishing the workers who keep Social Security working, who make sure our water is safe to drink, and who take care of our veterans.**
The people who are working at Social Security offices, still making sure seniors’ checks go out on time _won’t_ get their own check this week. Their bills didn’t stop. The rent is still due, and they still have families who need to eat. **We need solidarity, not charity.**
[**The Labor Force is distributing grocery cards to federal workers during this shutdown. clean_body: | | Peter, We reached out a few weeks ago asking you to help support the government workers who make our democracy happen. Today they missed another paycheck. The executive, legislative, and judiciary are leaving federal workers out in the cold. But that means it’s even more important for We the People to step up for our communities. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately, split between The Labor Force and United for Democracy: | | | | | | | | | | Thank you for supporting your fellow Americans, Stasha Rhodes United for Democracy Peter, Since Congress shut down on September 30, federal workers have either been off the job or working without pay. Today, they just missed their first paycheck. Instead of negotiating an end to the shutdown, Donald Trump and his administration have threatened mass firings, even pledging to renege on a law he signed to guarantee backpay to workers after a shutdown. Trump sees a path to political victory through punishing the workers who keep Social Security working, who make sure our water is safe to drink, and who take care of our veterans. The people who are working at Social Security offices, still making sure seniors’ checks go out on time won’t get their own check this week. Their bills didn’t stop. The rent is still due, and they still have families who need to eat. We need solidarity, not charity. If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately, split between The Labor Force and United for Democracy: | | | | | | | | | Federal employees are not bargaining chips. They are veterans, caregivers, law enforcement officers, and neighbors who serve their country and fellow Americans every day. name: James Talarico
subject: It’s been slower than expected.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Talarico for Texas
body:
#
Peter — I know you probably have a busy day ahead. So I’ll keep this short.
We expected fundraising to slow down this month as we stepped into a new quarter — but it’s slower than we had projected in our budget.
But if we’re going to win this race next November, we need to consistently be building the infrastructure to contact voters — on the doors, on TV, and through public events across the state.
We have no room to slow down.
So I’m trying to make up some ground to make sure we have the resources to take back Texas for the people.
What’s magical about our movement is that if enough people give just what they can, then it adds up quickly. This is a movement funded $5 and $25 at a time.
[**If you can contribute $5 or any amount you can spare, it would go a long way ahead of our October deadline. We have important goals to reach and can’t afford to falter this early.**](https://act.jamestalarico.com/go/512?c0=jt-em-launch.bF5RK3&refcode=em251025-1-ak&t=1&akid=189%2E1347274%2EeQp9pT)
_If you 've stored your info with ActBlue, we'll process your contribution instantly:_

James

name: JusticeDemocrats.com
subject: Shri Thanedar is spending taxpayer money on TV ads
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter,

Representative Shri Thanedar — the do-nothing Democrat being challenged by
working-class progressive Donavan McKinney — is spending taxpayer dollars
on TV ads.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251025.uQJGmY&refcode=em20251025-37138&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1002&akid=37138%2E1203867%2EafGTnX ]Tweet by Shane Goldmacher: Rep. Shri Thanedar (D-MI) is using taxpayer
House funds to run this TV ad about the shutdown... “Every American must
have affordable healthcare, and that’s why I’m fighting in Washington DC,
even if it means shutting this government down,” he says in it.

Instead of hiring caseworkers or legislative staff that could actually
help Detroit families, Shri is spending his taxpayer-funded office budget
on ads that boost his own political profile. Let’s be real: these are
basically taxpayer-funded campaign ads.

Don’t even get us started on the fact that he’s spending taxpayer dollars
on an ad about costs being too high...for taxpayers.

This isn't the first time Shri’s taken advantage of his budget to put
himself first. He has a history of running TV, radio, and other ads
(including gaudy billboards like this one) to boost his own fame while
Michigan suffers:

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251025.uQJGmY&refcode=em20251025-37138&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1003&akid=37138%2E1203867%2EafGTnX ]Photo of a Shri Thanedar billboard

Out-of-touch Democrats like Shri, who are more interested in vanity than
helping their working-class constituents, are exactly how our Party got
where it is today.

That’s why we've endorsed Donavan McKinney, a state representative running
to unseat Shri and bring real representation back to his neighbors in
MI-13.

Donavan is part of the next generation of Justice Democrats taking on
incumbents who refuse to fight for the working class with the vision and
urgency this moment demands. If we’re ever going to win Medicare for All,
a Green New Deal, or other big changes to level the playing field for
working families across our country, we need to elect leaders like Donavan
and send corporate shills like Shri packing.

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251025.uQJGmY&refcode=em20251025-37138&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1004&akid=37138%2E1203867%2EafGTnX ]Can you chip in $5 to Justice Democrats today to help us
support Donavan and the rest of our slate in their competitive primaries
next year?

[ https://go.justicedemocrats.com/go/140221?c0=jd-em-20251025.uQJGmY&refcode=em20251025-37138&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&t=1023&akid=37138%2E1203867%2EafGTnX ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

Justice Democrats

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20251025-37138-footer&t=1024&refcode2=37138_1203867_afGTnX&akid=37138%2E1203867%2EafGTnX

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to get involved. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://go.justicedemocrats.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1025&akid=37138%2E1203867%2EafGTnX

PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.
Justice Democrats
1390 Columbia Ave #261
Lancaster PA 17603
Email us: us@justicedemocrats.com

name: Nikki Budzinski
subject: House Republicans are out of touch
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Nikki for Congress
body:
Friend,

The government shutdown is in its fourth week, and House Republicans continue to be unwilling to come to the table. Affordable healthcare is on the line for thousands of Illinoisans and millions nationwide.

And while working people are missing paychecks and families are hurting, President Trump is spending $230 MILLION to gut the White House’s East Wing to build a ballroom.

It’s completely out of touch. Republicans’ priorities are clearly in the wrong place.

I’ve spent my life going to bat for Illinois. My grandparents – a union painter and a schoolteacher – inspired me to work hard and champion working families like ours. Before Congress, I fought for workers’ rights and led the effort to raise Illinois’ minimum wage to $15 an hour.

We’ve made progress since I flipped IL-13 from red to blue in 2022, but we’re facing truly unprecedented obstacles in Washington right now. I’m speaking out and doing all I can to deliver for Central and Southern Illinois, but real accountability is almost impossible without a Democratic majority.

Thank you,

Nikki

Donate >> https://secure.actblue.com/donate/nb-q4-2025?refcode=em-10.25.25-all-a

https://www.facebook.com/NikkiforCongress/?
https://www.instagram.com/nikkibudzinski/?
https://twitter.com/NikkiBudzinski?

Nikki is a champion for working families – from leading the fight to pass a $15 minimum wage in Illinois, to bringing manufacturing back to America. Now, she’s delivering in Congress for the people of Illinois' 13th Congressional District. Chip in to Nikki's campaign today.
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/nb-q4-2025?refcode=em-footer

subject: $10 for the last 10 days

But here's the truth: this race won't be decided by billionaires. It'll be decided by whether grassroots Democrats step up in these final 10 days.

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

What if I told you that $118,000 could potentially generate enough monthly income to cover all your retirement expenses?

Housing... healthcare... food... entertainment... emergencies.

While your neighbors need $1.5 million for the same lifestyle?

[See the proof that could change your retirement →](https://links.main.yourcapitalminds.com/u/click?_t=affa6bfca09a497f86454ba803e6a9a2&_m=02711abb55df4d9b8458d61c2155b25c&_e=pDZABzVSIaDjvSLjkRGL9pNiFlJ8sVPuqaZ8vZd2KQmSaxJD9aXEU_VX7el13hpCuWGtZzEgZOLfinXovuPRzIfR5dhzkmPWOmPwMBVzuluij_HTK1ZURwlS6NuqMNI5R_ffNBjgp_zYDSMvFf-T1fC8TCUepf53mh5EaXITIFTbsehMQKhY6qbcFAEbjjKIwRmZGE-9MnGLO_5Y_-nJuLHXoVVgqOnpHUIiNhu8uoOkWmxY5kFg6PA0EVRNT9jUAW4U5u1tt6Pa-zFQJdHnDP0IarCcqPWemVfbDB4ba449EdZPT--WmdCbYzYE_uwKDDd0Sp0qOUzIOhI4WKjvS81oG4kPbl6_Hvqjmf41xF3iv279fRmlJoPkk2VemEpwNgzqkqthUbTDg41ybpV_cerxPvOzy0PcMzXKuKoIuSxK4MYs463JTw1BK6dQSa7OMyNabo-HRhXw5X7EOhGl-B7YILL0BD1LbXyS2Up1prshQdhJIKLYE0zP0-aR4R0G9bGGHBQ34v39KSXz9WdI8dVLOGq55XWKk0k5I-QUHi1QOAYk3hO-5c5_ALjqRSyJ)

Tim Plaehn
Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. | | | What if I told you that $118,000 could potentially generate enough monthly income to cover all your retirement expenses? Housing... healthcare... food... entertainment... emergencies. While your neighbors need $1.5 million for the same lifestyle? Tim Plaehn Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from YourCapitalMinds.com, an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. name: Adam Schiff
subject: This is what patriotism looks like
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY SCHIFF FOR SENATE
body:
|
# [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/61?t=1&akid=9115%2E5257348%2EBP1R_4)
Last weekend, millions upon millions of people across the country — from our largest cities to our smallest towns — rallied at No Kings protests. The largest national demonstrations in U.S. history. Derek, last weekend was a reminder of what patriotism, free speech, and democracy really look like. [](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q42025_abs_em.dd-KUe&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20251025_AM1_V1_S1&t=4&akid=9115%2E5257348%2EBP1R_4) I thought a lot about my father, who passed away last year at 96 years old, when I attended the rally on the National Mall in Washington, D.C. My dad joined the service at the end of World War II. His generation, and those that have followed, have lived in a world that has seen our freedoms ever expanding across the globe. We saw new democracies being born. We saw walls coming down. We saw our freedoms expand. We saw our ability to associate with whomever we want to, to speak our piece, and to love who we love. For a long time, we thought that this expansion of rights and freedoms was inevitable. That it was, just as Martin Luther King Jr. said, the long arc of the universe always bending toward justice. Until suddenly, we found it not bending towards justice. We have learned that our incredible legacy of freedom and democracy is not inevitable. We have to defend it together. We have to speak out in support of our democracy. We have to say “hell no” to any wannabe king trying to tear it all down. What we saw across the country at the countless No Kings protests were millions of acts of patriotism. Of courage and decency, of people of all ages speaking out. Derek, that is what will save this country. Not a king, but the people. In solidarity, Adam[](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/1014?c0=q42025_abs_em.dd-KUe&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20251025_AM1_V1_S1&email=&t=5&akid=9115%2E5257348%2EBP1R_4) | | | [DONATE MONTHLY](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/2342?c0=q42025_abs_em.Iv4c2K&refcode=AU_ABS_FR_GEN_GEN_AC90_20251025_AM1_V1_S1&email=&t=6&akid=9115%2E5257348%2EBP1R_4)
---
135 E. Olive Ave., #750 Burbank CA, 91502
**[www.adamschiff.com](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/60?t=2&akid=9115%2E5257348%2EBP1R_4) | [contact@adamschiff.com](mailto:contact@adamschiff.com) | [Privacy Policy](https://act.adamschiff.com/go/64?t=3&akid=9115%2E5257348%2EBP1R_4)**

---

Text **JOIN** to **724433**. If you wish to no longer receive these messages, please [unsubscribe.](https://act.adamschiff.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=8&akid=9115%2E5257348%2EBP1R_4)

name: Michael Bennet
subject: the best part of this campaign
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Bennet for Governor
body:
Meeting folks on the campaign trail is the best part of this race.
| | |
---
**Friend, nothing beats traveling across Colorado, meeting face-to-face with people like you. It's the best part of this campaign.**
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117988473/578285962/851209409?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk931-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNGJiMGM2MS1iY2IxLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PfBFjL_KCjewRnpyJazHX5OPnpaAIWrk3OTJZu1dR1Q=)
If I'm going to be a governor who serves all Coloradans, I have to reach out to as many Coloradans as I can. So every chance I get to head home from D.C., I make sure to meet with folks from every corner of our state. I make sure to listen as they talk about their families, their jobs, their day-to-day priorities and their wildest dreams – and I’m so grateful to spend the next few days on the road.
**[To keep this grassroots movement trucking along in the governor's race, can you chip in $5 or more today?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117988474/578285963/851209409?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk931-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNGJiMGM2MS1iY2IxLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PfBFjL_KCjewRnpyJazHX5OPnpaAIWrk3OTJZu1dR1Q=)**

---
Now, don't get me wrong: **Delivering real, tangible results for Colorado over three terms in the U.S. Senate has been the honor of my life.**

And as long as I serve in this capacity, I'll stay laser-focused on pushing back against Trump and keeping up that track record.
**However, President Trump's lawless administration doesn’t care about delivering for the people of Colorado.** As he bulldozes the economy, our social safety nets and our democracy, Republicans look the other way – and too many in Washington treat it like politics as usual.
_That's_ why I’m coming back home to Colorado. The best solutions to our state's biggest issues can't come from Trump's crooked policies, and Washington D.C. is too broken to meet Coloradans' needs.
**Our solutions have to start here at home: in Colorado, by Colorado, for Colorado.**
I believe, together, we can create the solutions to our shared challenges.
I believe, together, we can forge better politics than what we see on cable news.
I believe, together, we can make our state the best place to raise a kid, start a business and retire with dignity.
And I believe that, together, Colorado can provide the vision both the Democratic Party and the entire country urgently need.
But making that happen will first require winning – **and since this campaign runs on people power, we're relying on committed folks like you to chip in $5, $10 or $15 at a time to keep us going full steam ahead and ensure we reach all of Colorado's 64 counties.**
**[So since I’m on the road again, friend, will you help fuel my campaign to victory by rushing the best contribution you can give today?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117988477/578285965/851209409?amounts=5,25,50,100,250&refcode=jtk931-fr-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjgvMS83NDc5MSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmNGJiMGM2MS1iY2IxLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=PfBFjL_KCjewRnpyJazHX5OPnpaAIWrk3OTJZu1dR1Q=)**

name: Ted Cruz
subject: We invoked the Nuclear Option
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Ted Cruz for Senate
body:

name: Team Fetterman
subject: 👉 you’ve been selected: health care focus group
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Fetterman for PA
body:
[ https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/51612?t=1001&akid=10386%2E434096%2EMSGhU4 ]John Fetterman

Derek, not one, not two, not three ... SIX former U.S. surgeons general – appointed by every Republican and Democratic president since George H. W. Bush – just banded together to issue a joint warning: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is putting Americans’ health at risk. 🚨

They're not taking this lightly. They know what it's like to work at the top of our nation's health department, they know RFK Jr. is a "driving force" behind our growing public health crisis, and they're sounding the alarm.

This comes right after 1,000 current and former HHS employees sent a blistering letter calling for RFK Jr.'s resignation.

And they're all saying the same thing: From funding cuts to dangerous anti-vaccine theories and so much more, RFK Jr.'s leadership is putting lives at risk.

John Fetterman voted NO on RFK Jr.'s confirmation way back in February. He stands with the surgeons general and the scientists and public health officials at HHS. We need steady, ethical leadership grounded in science, not someone who plays politics with their work.

So we have to know where you stand in the midst of all of this. We need [17] more responses from 18326 to complete our dataset, so please respond now:

How would you grade RFK Jr.'s job performance?

https://act.johnfetterman.com/go/51940?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&t=1002&akid=10386%2E434096%2EMSGhU4

Thanks for your quick response,

Team Fetterman 💛

name: Tim Walz
subject: I’m taking this to the people
disclaimer_text: paid for by Walz for Governor
body:
#
Peter,
Today, we’re hosting the first in a series of public town halls to discuss the desperate need for commonsense gun reform to save lives.
Gun violence and mass shootings have tragically taken too many lives and ravaged families across our state — with little to no legislative action to prevent them.
Communities and families demand change. A recent KSTP/SurveyUSA poll shows that 53 percent of respondents support an assault weapons ban, and 58 percent support a special session to address this violent epidemic.
**So I’m taking this issue directly to the people.**
Starting today, I’m hitting the road and hosting town halls in districts where state legislators are on the fence about supporting our proposed assault-style weapons and high-capacity magazines ban. These may be hard conversations, but they need to happen if we’re going to do what is necessary to stop further tragedies.
**[Minnesotans want and deserve this, Peter. As long as I’m governor, I’m going to keep fighting to make it happen. If you support me in this work, chip in $5 or whatever you can spare.](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251025.RiebKK&amount=5&refcode=em20251025-28&t=1&akid=28%2E34582%2EZ9dMti)**
**[Donate $5 ››](https://act.timwalz.com/go/73?c0=em_tw_20251025.RiebKK&amount=5&refcode=20251025-28&t=20&akid=28%2E34582%2EZ9dMti)**
With gratitude,
Tim

Under Governor Walz’s leadership, Minnesota is showing the world what’s possible when we come together with grit, compassion, and a shared sense of purpose.
We secured free school meals for every student, cut child poverty, passed Paid Family Leave, delivered record investments in education, and more.
**[Now we need your help to stop MAGA Republicans from dragging us backward. Pitch in $5 today to help us win this race and keep moving forward — together.](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_tw_footer?refcode=em20251025-28&t=21&refcode2=28_34582_Z9dMti&akid=28%2E34582%2EZ9dMti)**
**[Donate](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_tw_footer?refcode=em20251025-28&t=22&refcode2=28_34582_Z9dMti&akid=28%2E34582%2EZ9dMti)**
Email us: [info@walzforgovernor.org](mailto:info@walzforgovernor.org)
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way we keep in touch with people like you, so thank you for reading to the end.

St. Paul MN 55104

name: VoteVets.org
subject: 🪖 An important message for every VoteVets supporter... 🇺🇸
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
Peter - please, don’t click away before reading.
#
Peter - please, don’t click away before reading.
When we think about the people who have done the most to support VoteVets over the years, it’s not the people who write the biggest checks who come to mind.
It’s the people who were there when everything was on the line.
Like you, Peter.

Your support, voice, and participation have helped us run some of the most powerful and impactful ads out there. It has helped us continue to be one of the most impactful and effective organizations in politics, even with all that Trump throws our way.
Your support helps us win races that otherwise wouldn’t even be competitive.
It’s helped us fight for things that matter most to Veterans. It has stopped more efforts to privatize the VA than we can count. It won things like the PACT Act, ensuring Veterans suffering from toxic exposures could get the treatment they need and deserve.
And now, it’s a huge part of how we are fighting back against Trump and Doug Collins as they work to dismantle the VA, piece by piece.

But here’s an interesting fact: out of almost 2 million people who have taken action with VoteVets, only 1% have made a donation online to support our work.
Today, we’re asking you to join that select group.

Our average donation is $25. Some give more, many give less. But with Trump coming for everything we hold dear, all that matters is that you’re choosing to power our work with a donation.
We’ll put it right to work fighting and fighting back against these liars and hypocrites, and defending our Constitution at a moment when it is in extreme peril.
[**If helping VoteVets lead this fight from the front seems worth an $5 donation to you, we’re humbly asking you to make a donation before our official FEC deadline on October 31st. We really need you. Thank you.**](https://act.votevets.org/go/2927?c0=vv-pac-em-oct25.PLXLty&refcode=em251025-1-120&t=1&akid=34725%2E3013755%2EM8t59d)

name: Jeff Merkley
subject: Retaking the Democratic Senate Majority
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jeff Merkley for Oregon
body:
Sherrod Brown is in the fight for Ohio’s Senate seat in 2026, and I couldn’t be more thrilled to have him on the front lines with us.
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117989934/578290861/150430950?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDEvMS84OTMyNSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICIxOGZmZmRjNS1iZGIxLWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=QJ7YY5mVsV080Y3e9GzMYkbTsK34s0JOGaRQ_IevaY8=&emci=01edcd7e-07b1-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=18fffdc5-bdb1-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=7731215)
---

| | Sherrod Brown is in the fight for Ohio's Senate seat in 2026, and I couldn't be more thrilled to have him on the front lines with us. Sherrod is one of the toughest, most principled fighters for working people I've ever met. He's running because he refuses to let billionaires and corporate special interests write the rules — and because he believes, like I do, that every family deserves a fair shot. name: Powered by People
subject: We’re cutting through the chaos
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Powered by People, [poweredxpeople
body:
#
Early voting has started in the special election for Houston’s Congressional seat—but thanks to the GOP’s latest gerrymandering scheme, many voters will be shuffled into new districts for the next election. This comes as Greg Abbott has intentionally left the Democratic Congressional seat vacant for more than EIGHT months.
This confusion about district lines, polling places, and who’s representing where is no accident. This is the GOP’s playbook: confusion, delay, and disenfranchisement. But people power is stronger than their schemes.
Powered by People is on the ground to help—registering voters, knocking doors, delivering clear, accurate information so every eligible voter knows exactly how and where to make their voice heard.
It’s grassroots supporters like you who make it possible for us to organize in communities too often left behind, host town halls, register voters, and push back against the chaos the GOP is trying to create.
[**Will you chip in $3 to Powered by People today and help power this work forward? Together, we can cut through the confusion and make sure every vote counts.**](https://act.betoorourke.com/go/56109?c0=pbp-em-20251025.1mZlke&refcode=ema20251025-240425&amount=3&t=2&akid=240425%2E444211%2EKFnP_L)

# **_From Trump: You’ve got me worried_**
**__**

I’ve been trying to reach you for DAYS, and I’m starting to think that you’re ignoring me.
I really hope it’s because my messages haven’t been getting through to you.
Because the Fake News has been saying that MAGA support is at an ALL-TIME LOW...
_But I know you haven’t abandoned me._
I’m reaching out because **_YOUR NAME_** is at the top of my list of special MAGA Patriots, and I can’t afford to lose you.
**_[So could you take a quick second to reaffirm your support and give me your response to some important questions I have for you?](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e19037014/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUud2lucmVkLmNvbS90cnVtcC1uYXRpb25hbC1jb21taXR0ZWUtamZjL2xwLXByc3AtZW1haWwtMjAyNS13b3JyaWVkLXYxLXNiLw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEwMjU/LbmNseDFCdFlMWA/qP3V0bV9tZWRpdW09YnImdXRtX3Rlcm09MjE3NDVfd3Ix/gaPz5Qw/s1ka3b661c4)_**

[RESPOND TO TRUMP!](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e19037014/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUud2lucmVkLmNvbS90cnVtcC1uYXRpb25hbC1jb21taXR0ZWUtamZjL2xwLXByc3AtZW1haWwtMjAyNS13b3JyaWVkLXYxLXNiLw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEwMjU/LbmNseDFCdFlMWA/qP3V0bV9tZWRpdW09YnImdXRtX3Rlcm09MjE3NDVfd3Ix/gaPz5Qw/s1ka3b661c4)
**I know that you have never abandoned me and the MAGA Movement, but it’s been a while since I’ve heard from you...**
Please, I know that you’re a very busy person, but I could really use your response to some important questions that I have for you.
I have always taken into account YOUR thoughts, and I need them now more than ever.
[RESPOND TO TRUMP!](https://link.email.america1stwarroom.com/click/e19037014/VaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUud2lucmVkLmNvbS90cnVtcC1uYXRpb25hbC1jb21taXR0ZWUtamZjL2xwLXByc3AtZW1haWwtMjAyNS13b3JyaWVkLXYxLXNiLw/SZW1haWxfYW1lcmljYU9ORXN0d2Fycm9vbV9jb20yNTEwMjU/LbmNseDFCdFlMWA/qP3V0bV9tZWRpdW09YnImdXRtX3Rlcm09MjE3NDVfd3Ix/gaPz5Qw/s1ka3b661c4)
THANK YOU.
\- President Donald J. Trump

name: Chris Pappas
subject: This could be the most expensive race in NH history
disclaimer_text: Paid for by State Democrats Committee (StateDemocrats
body:
This just became one of the toughest and most expensive races in the 2026 election.
#
Peter, it’s Chris Pappas. I want you to hear it from me first: **John Sununu just entered the race for U.S. Senate in New Hampshire.**
That’s right. This just became one of the toughest and most expensive races in the 2026 election. **[So before I go any further, I have to ask: Will you split $15 or more between my campaign and State Democrats Committee today?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/96920?refcode=em20251025-9625&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=1&akid=9625%2E614464%2EzMG030)**
**[Chip in $15](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/96920?refcode=em20251025-9625&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=2&akid=9625%2E614464%2EzMG030)**
National Republicans spent months recruiting John Sununu because they know he’s their best shot at flipping this Senate seat.
Sununu first went to Washington over a quarter of a century ago—then he cashed in, making millions while New Hampshire paid the price for his record. Now he’s running to do it again, and this time he’ll have megadonors and special interests powering his campaign.
**Reporters are predicting that this race could be one of the most expensive in New Hampshire history.** But I know our grassroots movement is stronger than the corporate interests that have propped Sununu up — and this is the moment to prove it. We need to show them that people power still wins in New Hampshire.
**[Will you split a donation right now between me and State Democrats Committee to show Sununu and the GOP that this seat belongs to the people — not to corporate special interests?](https://act.lucaskunce.com/go/96920?refcode=em20251025-9625&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&amounts=5%2C20%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&t=3&akid=9625%2E614464%2EzMG030)**
If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly:

name: MCG Red Dot
subject: No Parallax. No Guesswork. Just Unmatched Speed
body:
The Stinger When Every Second Counts...
| | | Unsubscribe [here](https://links.nationalgunowner.org/a/1537/click/22095/6374453/8227c823ae6c05bc7559e091ec6638eb2326e33b/bffc0ab47c25c44ea0e5914a07c4e5dca7f06ed6). We'll miss you - so will great opportunities.

---

---
---
|
---
If you’re like me, then you don’t need to put a bullet through a dime at 300 yards very often...
BUT when you do, you want an optic that rises to the challenge and threads that needle.

---
[](https://links.nationalgunowner.org/a/1537/click/22095/6374453/8ced1fb0468f454e97887b40480befdb49220fd9/bffc0ab47c25c44ea0e5914a07c4e5dca7f06ed6)
---
| | |
---
That’s why I’m so f-cking excited about this red dot I’m about to share with you.

It’s called the **[Stinger Combat Sight](https://links.nationalgunowner.org/a/1537/click/22095/6374453/653a0ef1881c64c26ca6283c120318a11e959e6b/bffc0ab47c25c44ea0e5914a07c4e5dca7f06ed6)** and it harnesses technology developed by the US Military to deliver the tightest groups possible even in the worst conditions.

The Stinger is not just similar to what SEAL Team 6 used to take out Bin Laden. It’s identical.
---
---
And get this. It’s actually better than that famous optic... Because this one is equipped with both RED and GREEN laser holograph reticles.

But that’s not even the best part. The best part is that you can now get this for UNDER $80. And did I mention I'm covering the shipping during this 3 day event?

Better hurry, I've only got 200 of these sights in the warehouse so it's first come, first served.

**[Grab your Stinger before they're all gone!](https://links.nationalgunowner.org/a/1537/click/22095/6374453/653a0ef1881c64c26ca6283c120318a11e959e6b/bffc0ab47c25c44ea0e5914a07c4e5dca7f06ed6)** P.S. I'm positive we'll run out by the end of the weekend, so if you want one of these you'd better get on it like a hobo on a ham sandwich!
---
[](https://links.nationalgunowner.org/a/1537/click/22095/6374453/8ced1fb0468f454e97887b40480befdb49220fd9/bffc0ab47c25c44ea0e5914a07c4e5dca7f06ed6)

name: Marc Elias
subject: Let me explain -
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC | PO Box 96047, Washington, DC 20090 | **[dscc
body:
| | | Hey there, it's Marc Elias.

For years, I've gone to court to stop Trump and defend democracy -- and I've won 64 times. **Now, working with the DSCC, we're doing our part to safeguard voting rights, uphold the rule of law, and protect your freedom.**

But I won't lie: Trump's actions put all that work at risk -- and we need to fight back NOW. [**Support from grassroots Democrats like you is what powers the fight to flip the Senate so Democrats can put a stop to Trump's extremism. Will you give $5 to the DSCC to help us stop Trump's allies by winning back a Democratic Senate majority?**](https://go.dscc.org/go/22074?amount=5&t=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20251025-MarcElias-EliasStandingUpToTrump-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20251025-MarcElias-EliasStandingUpToTrump-Pros-sl1&akid=21583%2E9716520%2EcZWstE)

Let me explain further:

Donald Trump is ignoring due process and openly mocking the courts. He's threatening the very foundation of our democracy by attacking fair, free, and secure elections. And he's going after voting rights -- the very bedrock of our democracy.

Worst of all, Trump has a seemingly **endless** supply of fools and cowards in the Republican Party who are willing to help him.

But the DSCC and I are fighting back: The _moment_ Trump signed an executive order to interfere in our elections and threaten voting rights, we filed suit against him on behalf of Democrats nationwide. And the good news is, we've already notched some key victories in the case!

**But the fight isn't over yet. Donald Trump is an aspiring despot -- not just for a single day -- and despots _never_ willingly give up their power. We have to take it from them.**

Winning the Senate is the first step, and Democrats need to flip just four seats to claim the majority and wrest power away from the GOP.

**As I've said before, the fight for democracy is the fight of our generation.[Right now, the best thing YOU can do is chip in $5 to the DSCC to stand in this fight with me to unseat Senate Republicans and elect a Democratic Senate majority the first chance we get >>](https://go.dscc.org/go/22074?amount=5&t=6&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&addr2=&city=Cresco&state=Pennsylvania&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&ask=5&amounts=5%2C10%2C20%2C50&code=DPE-FD-20251025-MarcElias-EliasStandingUpToTrump-Pros-sl1&refcode=DPE-FD-20251025-MarcElias-EliasStandingUpToTrump-Pros-sl1&akid=21583%2E9716520%2EcZWstE)**

We need your help to fight for the Senate. If you've saved payment info with ActBlue Express, your donation will be processed immediately.

name: Brent Hennrich
subject: Remembering Addison: Forever in our hearts
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Brent Hennrich for Washington
body:
| | [ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMU_BeZcmRBr16dPjlrMKhhjiC51ataw3Peft1wIO87ITSxjytRqnk5Se65PvvR0SC1Vi60eIcmGaf4kR6MPJnbRGd2XwIrhDn8ZZ-Z4PPuO0Vx2PdmM8zh_l0Ht__s0eBM3mD7hm90kTQelU6xsIYvzfbSfygYrZZUjsxov9maqLhfLFNDUmtxe7eUx6yaZQZM80_dRUTbKB_6TgbUfJdX1a2vZDIWK4WQbJor2-01Ns/4l1/HN-ghim0RI6nOm8lqL5rSg/h0/h001.T4L7y1xgEUQTsphVkvqoPv8BcMx2wcAz4aIi3CuWVbI)
---
Peter,
13 years ago today, my wife Amber and I lost our daughter Addison, as I had to decide in seconds to abort due to pregnancy complications. About a month earlier, Amber started bleeding and was rushed to the ER. I was out of state, working in Alaska at the time, and rushed home to return to her side. We learned she had suffered a placental abruption - a dangerous and often deadly pregnancy complication. That began a month-long journey of racing from one specialist to the next. We were scared, but we were hopeful. We followed every directive - and the doctors and specialists did everything they could to help us welcome Addison safely into the world. That split second decision was the hardest one I've ever made, and it was the right one for our family.
---
| [ ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv6r8GR7zKbLdKY8JcRsBH8aQhQzL-6XFLiJ1IzrYZqHcVu3_2xEWSSvdjjDmkiSUCN0PN9yohfZYUd4_lfYIVkiAEjtN5pu7nisxy6bFk-C7Lfbwz7uJaztts4xWsOY8GckS0nStSnPL4nisQ5BNcD1Eb-F2T-DdruoEpsoa4qRoA8FfWVsNEN5PRenh_Y6dCDrLlfD6Z07pIjWiqPfmj-O8wg_d9J6GgqIueRs1qKEiInoIeWrQiUQL3ORxgCX7u5LiY6Wq3Tpk31cMvptKUSKtq6v04uzBGJQM0Hp9cuAVfZAqf3NORaSFx0VxEoySpWHrrCR9HGDVq8AQH9amZ_St2-UwmxG_6_NTYtmuA7Qw/4l1/HN-ghim0RI6nOm8lqL5rSg/h1/h001.sdiKe8pLChPQ81uMnvt46ne7Q6Bk688W9FkJGlNXRsw)
---
Addison was a badly wanted child. Our daughter Briana was so excited to become a big sister. If I had been prevented from making that call in the exact moment it had to be made, I would have ended up a single father with a toddler. I would have lost the love of my life. Our son Braydon and our daughter Brynlee never would've been born. Our family would have been incomplete. That's why, even though this is a sad day, it is still a day that my family celebrates. Reproductive care saved Amber's life and gave me our family. But today, those rights are under attack like never before.
---
[ **HELP ME FIGHT FOR REPRODUCTIVE RIGHTS** ](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv6r8GR7zKbLdKY8JcRsBH8aQhQzL-6XFLiJ1IzrYZqHcVu3_2xEWSSvdjjDmkiSUCN0PN9yohfZYUd4_lfYIVkiAEjtN5pu7nisxy6bFk-C6_Q6JcqVGtEEmCsZ9WKtQ4fSB7oJNl712pr5IzgYWzxO6_uJ4Tr2YHYIbp2gZvPhM8G21chTqcs1a9hzY_E_q1jZdk2W2euQQJZLBdOjd6XiK8uIEpQxh7WNfRSOxpSofn8gOMSu8j16Q0HkiEVIe4oqIxzdDDvS1HElkgN2yHLh_-ck_peLjzAbpHAz53ABnMeF382vXli3P-Z4E2JWB7PHADd2bpiZ8hxBcBDGtElUJYvYg5P6A5c47XEAJmZBw/4l1/HN-ghim0RI6nOm8lqL5rSg/h2/h001.tFf2qXs8-lmczJVTEijQdRbcOTal1o4ikSL3YaANLLY)
---
In more than half of U.S. states today, women do not have the same freedom Amber did to terminate a pregnancy for any reason. Even in so-called "life of the mother" exceptions, the process is bogged down by lawmakers and lawyers. And you may think you're safe in a blue state like Washington - but with extremists like Mike Johnson at the helm in Congress, and bipartisan seeking MGP, abortion rights are on the line nationally. MGP has voted multiple times with the Republicans to restrict access to reproductive care via 2 NDAA's, and just days into her first term voted for HCR3 that said the federal government "recognizes the sanctity of life and the important role pro-life facilities, groups, and churches play in supporting pregnant women, infants, and families." Trump's FDA has recently begun "reviewing the safety" of mifepristone, one of the two medications needed for a safe, non-surgical abortion that has been approved and used safely for over twenty years. It's not enough for them that women across the country are fleeing across state lines in search of adequate care. **They want to make sure that women have nowhere to flee.** Anti-choice Republicans won't rest until families like mine are forced to do the unthinkable: watch women die for unviable pregnancies. **For me, the question of abortion rights is not a question at all. There is zero room for compromise on this issue. Reproductive care is between a patient and their provider, END OF DISCUSSION!** [**If you agree that there is no room to compromise on abortion rights, then join our campaign and help me fight for every woman 's right to control her own body.**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqv6r8GR7zKbLdKY8JcRsBH8aQhQzL-6XFLiJ1IzrYZqHcVu3_2xEWSSvdjjDmkiSUCN0PN9yohfZYUd4_lfYIVkiAEjtN5pu7nisxy6bFk-C560oasLNMH-Oc5z3oOg7nZKNjlfSK2Ykt2z_RnbnKnEAbSePYGpPvkQgsYSM_JNnGrEORUjbr1xEVnDrJsg18tY_rcXFC3Rm6nJ6K4c1IybW9-TEtIhbJLDp4dzxXORzGPLmPaMm2RymfwlVi82ApCpk8UgYKjF7-csx-gTohuZ83Lr0dQTqNHv6CmcqNSWY4ilNzM8tmCT9zUXgY88ICyrvX9_0VnLFrT1B3J596bln0fZPexR7FKTzQcGFHG1xg/4l1/HN-ghim0RI6nOm8lqL5rSg/h3/h001.zE96ZUmnQoAqK0ZdLnZvQTjllR0ixkUzNzHHdPKM8fw)

clean_body: | | Peter, 13 years ago today, my wife Amber and I lost our daughter Addison, as I had to decide in seconds to abort due to pregnancy complications. About a month earlier, Amber started bleeding and was rushed to the ER. I was out of state, working in Alaska at the time, and rushed home to return to her side. We learned she had suffered a placental abruption - a dangerous and often deadly pregnancy complication. That began a month-long journey of racing from one specialist to the next. We were scared, but we were hopeful. We followed every directive - and the doctors and specialists did everything they could to help us welcome Addison safely into the world. That split second decision was the hardest one I've ever made, and it was the right one for our family. | Addison was a badly wanted child. Our daughter Briana was so excited to become a big sister. If I had been prevented from making that call in the exact moment it had to be made, I would have ended up a single father with a toddler. I would have lost the love of my life. Our son Braydon and our daughter Brynlee never would've been born. Our family would have been incomplete. That's why, even though this is a sad day, it is still a day that my family celebrates. Reproductive care saved Amber's life and gave me our family. But today, those rights are under attack like never before. In more than half of U.S. states today, women do not have the same freedom Amber did to terminate a pregnancy for any reason. Even in so-called "life of the mother" exceptions, the process is bogged down by lawmakers and lawyers. And you may think you're safe in a blue state like Washington - but with extremists like Mike Johnson at the helm in Congress, and bipartisan seeking MGP, abortion rights are on the line nationally. MGP has voted multiple times with the Republicans to restrict access to reproductive care via 2 NDAA's, and just days into her first term voted for HCR3 that said the federal government "recognizes the sanctity of life and the important role pro-life facilities, groups, and churches play in supporting pregnant women, infants, and families." Trump's FDA has recently begun "reviewing the safety" of mifepristone, one of the two medications needed for a safe, non-surgical abortion that has been approved and used safely for over twenty years. It's not enough for them that women across the country are fleeing across state lines in search of adequate care. They want to make sure that women have nowhere to flee. Anti-choice Republicans won't rest until families like mine are forced to do the unthinkable: watch women die for unviable pregnancies. For me, the question of abortion rights is not a question at all. There is zero room for compromise on this issue. Reproductive care is between a patient and their provider, END OF DISCUSSION! Let's Heal Washington! Brent Hennrich | | Marie Gluesenkamp-Perez has betrayed Democrats and the voters of WA-3 by repeatedly voting with MAGA against women's rights, immigrants, and more. Brent Hennrich is a lifelong Washingtonian, a proud working class dad, and lifelong Democratic activist. name: Rashida Tlaib
subject: Here’s how we strengthen democracy at all levels of government
body:
Elections are coming up Tuesday Nov 4 and I’m endorsing local public servants. | | |
---
Friend, Did you know that local and state elections are coming up soon? Don’t forget to vote on or by November 4th! In this time of federal gridlock, we’ve got to lift up champions for the people at all levels of government. We need more elected leaders who are rooted in their communities, and who will strengthen our democracy through deeply listening to their constituents. So I’m proudly endorsing progressive leaders running for public office at the local level, where they can make huge impacts in our communities. [**Today I want to highlight two people-powered candidates running in my district who need our support:**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtiu64DAa6R6mEIlUl6sWbtQhnH0-JwGUJhHAp7pimN6WyWfIv5f8rB_vm8VkMcwQ1NZktCmg7m-QRTwgpyQ0LO4xOPLGsacxuOBq0MxlDcZ_iiQoL4NuhjwmsQdiWdXL0nr19G3_7OZaIjcKAYt5n8tdUMG4-4fWCVxv7ZaQKA2TV9L3khG3vltvcsYCrD8pQP0np79WdZYjBTyytGBfIPyhHF7WGIh9eSxxwSxK_j51BxD56bfYqmWZ9DW2dqsgHWTsLQAyk2krOleV6PG97fIu1VMzAiqsVb5f8x_5Y9iN6wGqKdVDS5bJT72a3nKY9UShgYmxMT_ad3n3EtvL9JoOfZejHirSlSinUL6lYjNK8sgdZZNAURR__f2LP_LedewGOaFwulONNWZmEDGUTa/4l1/5r30bijiRfq4--0m95KVkA/h0/h001.Yi0rzdlZivBSiFfzqXLrYUybpyKe1nWzILCv0iukbAs) **Stacy Jackson, who’s running for Southfield City Council to be a voice for the people.** She’s been a grassroots leader serving her community for years as a champion for housing rights, equity and social justice, ending gun violence, better infrastructure, and a more responsive government. When I joined Stacy to knock on doors last week, her love for Southfield residents shone through as she spoke with her neighbors: [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtiu64DAa6R6mEIlUl6sWbtQhnH0-JwGUJhHAp7pimN6WZoOLbWMUsAkc1Fxx3fv8NJEZtYzpKBvlLtv0pM7VUuzJaGMBqqCxaEMUu6xKW0tm2BIj_5_gpDCiHzm6ekl9QkarQW4nmL8XiHthGZYXPN8xqfh-8459nyYZIcvRR2aBykptgx7hh53vbHrvL6ZfQapQViGQV7INcixO5vwSKJjL4PUHLf8Ae39YISw4VBKqrZQCiVWOTyqjky9OYC8A2xBe10c4LgJGN7jgYaQKC44aIPpjOmJ2gllShO7h-a5QJ9lj_SJozp4nWwLp3sbWzui4g2UtWL5a1EGnN6ohiLsppjy_amwF9FMZLTNkfDbrOQFL7W0RTDMcOW6pSOWreGDv_CooaqrhXwc-Sbya9I/4l1/5r30bijiRfq4--0m95KVkA/h1/h001.0Fy262Q6Ghh44TcjxnnkUZ-uiyvtYMwvDpUpARKDsMo) **I’m also proud to endorse Lisa Hicks-Clayton, who’s running for re-election as the Treasurer of Dearborn Heights**. Lisa goes above and beyond to serve her community, including running food, clothing, and school supply drives for people in need. She regularly holds office hours and constituent service days, helping thousands of constituents with tax and water assistance, home foreclosure prevention, and free financial literacy programs. [](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtiu64DAa6R6mEIlUl6sWbtQhnH0-JwGUJhHAp7pimN6cb5h4jeA8qM9RX218eBII2Bh4VZCrpGjzZOf29EdQnKsiOelOctrTz2eg00AML7prEGb03NIquAfk13JD_pzPORtA0M3f2J8ZEF4mbGMQirXGbTb45hETAGKutdBWfHHhe8_1y8f_fHI-iWe6yI9dNqGo6CZzIRnsgBTIncgpfP976MA3S19YU169b6Bizii3ArfcEbJN5KKSUv4Wkyvjbs_mqGGfCwPNmRiwpNs78_9G-57GBcc-yqzjiUy-l5r3ACWWr2swEGIWis-z2dntpFOrQWbyS60G3TSywiB-PZq-rCtXL4JeJP89I7_ZwrgYakVPY3l1sDKfhjqHpxRbjbS3_DSUuKxcI4LG5dVyEY/4l1/5r30bijiRfq4--0m95KVkA/h2/h001.Tus31gEWAAcP-cKWasLWW5Sjk0owy7WASB7Z9BvQBMY) **Both Lisa and Stacy are committed to building trust in government through transparency. We need them and leaders like them in government! But they’re running in competitive races, and with people voting right now by absentee ballot and early in-person voting, they urgently need our support.** [**Please chip in what you can afford right now to support progressive leaders and dedicated public servants Lisa Hicks-Clayton and Stacy Jackson, as well as my Rooted In Community Leadership PAC—which trains and supports progressive candidates like them!**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtiu64DAa6R6mEIlUl6sWbtQhnH0-JwGUJhHAp7pimN6fYOsLwB1GuXwhhUUHRG5C67bMf4yMerRojEYcqlczqsCZPBDX8DD9AvImaM3OYNlCA1SmSCnp11WVdY9gQe_USQ8CEPBrZWgjjLYPLLCtlM4vWgCa_tdyFo3ElEPlKLPt9b8O3MBRr67JU0EyYNlceONFX8Z-WDjaF2H3EFVXfHu4hWv3XncQks0X7LkH-HcGizKviZ8LlptEiTcHAk9-HiGWQzsT4iIe-8Y7p5LDj1-4F0R9zhSQnFvGOa0wnNQgevhRH2wbaqUnzBfYrcU4trep3xxeZOFTb5QPloSoYglA0Z_SEX_tGOyHee_ZmQwNvBbMUrQ5yc_N7s6rRRR-He9hWNpUQ5cD5Mkrl5tpcA/4l1/5r30bijiRfq4--0m95KVkA/h3/h001._NLxroXiqKn9AkMD1RcwcTyBiGi_yBGW8lddNE9eGrg) _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your secure donation will go through immediately, split evenly between Stacy Jackson and Rooted in Community PAC. Like me, Stacy Jackson grew up in Southwest Detroit and experienced environmental injustice firsthand. She’s lost relatives to health issues due to pollution, which sparked her passion for environmental justice. If elected, she’ll be a much-needed fighter for our rights—from our right to clean water and air, to healthcare and education, and constitutional rights such as free speech.
A fighter for working families, Lisa Hicks-Clayton has been endorsed by unions like AFSCME Michigan and the Michigan Regional Council of Carpenters and Millwrights. Lisa also serves the people as a food pantry manager who delivers food to people who are home-bound, and as a board member for two local organizations: the Western Wayne County NAACP and the Dearborn-Dearborn Heights League of Women Voters.
[](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtiu64DAa6R6mEIlUl6sWbtQhnH0-JwGUJhHAp7pimN6SePTc8vYV7J-OXOLg1OHKskcruptLYS04y9ii2te5uAkpXGZHOl9qyb09sac3X522bgJRvu9vfdf3lwmSosN-4D_VGlIMWxhLfVZvJfcp9F2Gi_HfukdUiKoif1kc5guKdzSrq-Fc6bDaTWzcK4PZ6YRtfI_foyBsHsdUjCqgnda_hDwb2xqFf8xHPN7W40xx5kxUxRMfVIrtdgmjaQJM7Xi9cvLoQyYFFy49HfIYl5qpsC1uHMBz8pUoIBvT5z0KHdE7HO9_C6P3esrfGPrVkBa31smf7DveaYle3W1olei-Q9EYDQtk30xFkfmr_dxtkT-YWMNMftKDmlcJaVgT31HoORmfUmQaFjRBLZD1DP/4l1/5r30bijiRfq4--0m95KVkA/h10/h001.uZZrs-E50nHIMfQhgV6taNhX6-tz-C5NtDHQGlIBs_g)
_Photo of Lisa Hicks-Clayton_
In addition, Lisa has done such a great job as treasurer that the Wayne County Treasurers Association elected her President, and the Michigan Municipal League selected her to join their intensive training program for elected women leaders.
**Both Lisa and Stacy are the fighters for the people that our communities need and deserve. With a little over one week left until Election Day, and with absentee ballots in voters’ hands and early in-person voting available in many MI communities, these candidates urgently need our support.**
[**Can you pitch in today to support these extraordinary public servants as well as my Rooted In Community Leadership PAC (which supports and trains other exceptional candidates)?**](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtiu64DAa6R6mEIlUl6sWbtQhnH0-JwGUJhHAp7pimN6QBS4kAysOg-5KxN5HfOeqJL3SfDY0Io83kvmiAlOHeaYwjHH_fOBCJ9YImf6mZgwlUD5S-M1-92CAzTCsIJIWvIAnlrBD7ufGIZhCwhtxV7VIte7Emt3VbE50DJPIc-u2CG_DdGW2IDzKeiUAHdmrL1l_qU8JFkBfy_3GqTQIzhRvq1wcekqJTVvF6k5iKk5Pz1H98-xVrzX6if8wWpNRUpCBGArpiPpT4Ae4coPfeM5ykNatXUDjA22_T-_wwN2jYI3EykbHdA1dAqmMFu5VMuyOzrUTdr2HggnIF02NIEOvpiP56Uf9eylBOfL5n_AyX-J0kBZ4J3aWXKQDXmgR6npk3h2RBHOrD2d-4cBXNi/4l1/5r30bijiRfq4--0m95KVkA/h11/h001.8xaBo4nJUgKSWRnc5kLHThM0KV5oKBTQ_YGfhLmu4ow)
[RUSH A DONATION TODAY](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqtiu64DAa6R6mEIlUl6sWbtQhnH0-JwGUJhHAp7pimN6YE7oryqqZa90Kc8cTeu59pbtzKLPU0tzlu8PNSsTx9SPLZh2qEuG-DGaEb-YggmJDUVoGkJL5dW-Xxvd9ImAkpIyFXGNuBppFo6LDS7z6o3ZXubz54YtEZ_CUy3vYMrqCDen7cm-9SHlJZFBoyv7U4fMLgEJsPC_8OfmZwCrL6CFLYeHVpBgV3SMwDZytgByV7IwbXaQn4uL4BtpSUTGLNojNOPCYYL7j2N07mELSYLr1Udg6xlZXs1aWcbhUeX2S1q2SHWtkdAwm7kUdEM9c_6qc3d9tx_mSx-pLBjk1p1yE6SSlJQ0EhMRXaq5QBfWxDEoF0GAXXe1m2GsMFY_StBGdTjPip5lI2Dw0dYEzof/4l1/5r30bijiRfq4--0m95KVkA/h12/h001.oDn9U940rrsFmRG6WwMJQRC3sTcKfKLrUS4re4RUyuQ)

Rashida
